mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals an extended monetary accommodation regime. Under this regime, equity valuations expand due to compressed discount rates and reduced opportunity cost of equity capital relative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during ZLB periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market momentum and rising household income expectations. This drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth fails to precede positive equity returns in > 40% of 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,028k and RSAFS at 442,931M as of 2014-11
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) contraction of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. This reduces corporate profit margins and forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO contracts > 0.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This weakens aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth below 1.5% combined with FEDFUNDS near zero indicates deflationary pressure and monetary accommodation. This environment supports equity valuations through low real discount rates and reduced inflation risk premium, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.03 and FEDFUNDS at 0.12% as of 2014-11
failure_condition: Joint condition of low inflation and near-zero rates fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter at an annualized rate above 2.5% signals sustained economic expansion. This expansion supports corporate revenue growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days in the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to precede positive equity returns in > 45% of 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth signals synchronized supply and labor capacity expansion. When both series show positive momentum simultaneously, this indicates the economy is operating near potential with rising productivity and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.66 and PAYEMS at 140,347k as of 2014-11
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.
